pr-evidence/databricks-stage-submit-dry-.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5/high reviewer Hume reported no findings across correctness, token/secret hygiene, Databricks dry-run/stage-submit behavior, public API compatibility, docs, and tests; noted only a non-blocking direct CLI negative-test gap.
verification: python -m pytest -q tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_repository_hygiene.py -> 123 passed
PYTHONPATH=src python -m document_kv_cache.databricks_runs ... stage-and-submit --dry-run on the saved g6.8xlarge payload -> wrote ok stage-and-submit-plan sidecar with no token-like strings
installed-wheel cachet-databricks-runs ... stage-and-submit --dry-run with DATABRICKS_HOST/TOKEN unset -> passed
GitHub CI Test and build for PR #308 -> SUCCESS
what_changed: Added plan_databricks_stage_and_submit and cachet-databricks-runs stage-and-submit --dry-run to validate staged DBFS artifacts and summarize submit payloads without Databricks credentials or network requests.
Shared stage-and-submit artifact preparation between dry-run planning and real uploads so strict payload DBFS coverage, local file validation, size limits, and DBFS path validation stay consistent.
Exposed the dry-run API through Cachet and legacy facades, and documented no-network preflight examples for benchmark and engine-probe payloads.
pr-evidence/databricks-stage-submit-dry-.why: The g6/L4 Databricks launch path needs a token-safe preflight that proves runner and wheel staging is complete before any Databricks upload or runs/submit request is attempted.
